pattern: Tracking the largest and smallest running subarray sums via two Kadane-style passes yields the maximum absolute subarray sum in one scan.
hints: The absolute sum is large when a subarray is either very positive or very negative, so consider both directions.
Run Kadane's algorithm once for the maximum subarray sum and once for the minimum subarray sum.
The answer is the larger of the maximum running sum and the negation of the minimum running sum; allowing an empty subarray keeps both bounds at zero or better.

edge_inputs.description: best is a positive run in the middle
best is a negative run whose magnitude wins
all negative, full array gives largest magnitude
all positive, full array is the answer

stress.description: moderate signed array across the value range
